title: IRGC Budget Nearly Double That of Regular Army
detail: Budget analysis reveals the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps received 311 trillion tomans ($6 billion) in 2025, nearly twice the regular Army's allocation of 177 trillion tomans ($3.4 billion). Critics argue the disparity reflects the regime's prioritization of ideological defense and proxy operations over conventional military readiness, at a time when the June war exposed critical gaps in Iran's air defense and conventional capabilities. Reformist media outlets cite the figures as evidence of misplaced spending priorities amid economic collapse.

title: US Sanctions Designations Against Iran Surpass All Previous Years
detail: The US Office of Foreign Assets Control has designated more entities tied to Iran in 2025 than in any single year since the original 2018 maximum pressure campaign. Over 875 persons, vessels, and aircraft have been sanctioned as part of the administration's expanded enforcement campaign targeting Iran's shadow banking, money laundering, weapons proliferation, and sanctions evasion networks. The State Department names Iran the 'chief destabilizing force' in the world.

title: Rolling Blackouts Enter Second Consecutive Winter
detail: Iranian provinces report 3-4 hours of daily power outages as the energy crisis enters its second winter. Aging infrastructure, reduced fuel imports under snapback sanctions, and damage from the June war to power generation facilities compound the problem. Provincial governors appeal for emergency natural gas allocations as temperatures drop below freezing in northwestern Iran, while hospitals in multiple cities continue operating on backup generators.
